questions.text: Process by which unrelated species evolve to functionally resemble each other is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Convergent evolution is the right option. In convergent evolution, similar phenotypes occur in distantly related taxa.
In divergent evolution, different species have evolved from common ancestors in different habitats. They are homologous in structure.
Coevolution is the process of reciprocal evolutionary change that occurs between pairs of species as they interact with one another.
In parallel evolution, similar phenotypes occur in closely related taxa.

questions.explanation: The pattern of evolution in which different species have evolved from different ancestors in a common habitat is known as convergent evolution. Such organisms have similar functions but differ in structures.

questions.text: In the absence of a catalyst, ammonia burns in an excess of oxygen to produce steam and nitrogen. What is the volume of oxygen remaining when 60 cm³ of ammonia is burnt in 100 cm³ of oxygen, all volumes being measured at the same temperature and pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as it doesn't match the calculated value.
This option is incorrect as it doesn't match the calculated value.
This option is incorrect as it doesn't match the calculated value.

4NH3 + 3O2→ 6H2O + 2N2 60 cm³ 100 cm³ : Given (22,400 × 4) cm³ of ammonia uses (22,400 × 3) cm³ of O2 1 cm³ of ammonia uses .75 cm³ of O2 60 cm³ (given) of ammonia will use .75 × 60= 45 cm³ of O2 Hence the remaining volume of O2 will be 100-45=55 cm³

questions.explanation: 4NH3 + 3O2→ 6H2O + 2N2 60 cm³ 100 cm³ : Given (22,400 × 4) cm³ of ammonia uses (22,400 × 3) cm³ of O2 1 cm³ of ammonia uses .75 cm³ of O2 60 cm³ (given) of ammonia will use .75 × 60= 45 cm³ of O2 Hence the remaining volume of O2 will be 100-45=55 cm³
